scenarios.name: Specific Performance of Sale Agreement

checks.detail: Specific Relief Act / specific performance cited
At least one statutory section cited
Prayer for execution of sale deed
No invented amounts (10L/50L never in facts)

scenarios.draft_text:
IN THE COURT OF THE CITY CIVIL COURT
AT Bengaluru

O.S. No. __________ / 20____

Anita Sharma,
Aged about 52 years,
Occupation: {{PLAINTIFF_OCCUPATION}},
Residing at: Indiranagar, Bengaluru.

                                                        ... PLAINTIFF

                    VERSUS

Rajesh Reddy,
Aged about 60 years,
Occupation: {{DEFENDANT_OCCUPATION}},
Residing at: HSR Layout, Bengaluru.

                                                        ... DEFENDANT

SUIT FOR SPECIFIC PERFORMANCE OF CONTRACT WITH COSTS

MOST RESPECTFULLY SHOWETH:

JURISDICTION

1. The Plaintiff is a person sui juris and competent to institute the present suit. The Defendant is also a person sui juris and can be sued.

2. This Hon'ble Court has territorial jurisdiction to try and entertain the present suit inasmuch as the cause of action arose within the local limits of the jurisdiction of this Hon'ble Court at Bengaluru, and the Defendant carries on business / resides within such territorial limits.

3. This Hon'ble Court has pecuniary jurisdiction to try the present suit, the suit being valued at Rs. {{TOTAL_SUIT_VALUE}}/- (Rupees {{TOTAL_SUIT_VALUE_WORDS}} Only), which is within the pecuniary limits prescribed for this Hon'ble Court.

COMMERCIAL COURTS ACT COMPLIANCE

4. The present suit involves a commercial dispute of specified value (exceeding Rs. 10,00,000/-). The Plaintiff has complied with the mandatory pre-institution mediation requirement under Section 12A of the Commercial Courts Act, 2015, [OR] The Plaintiff seeks urgent interim relief exempting pre-institution mediation under Section 12A of the Commercial Courts Act, 2015.

LIMITATION

5. The present suit is within the period of limitation prescribed under Article 54 of the Schedule to the Limitation Act, 1963, which provides a period of three years from date fixed for performance, or if no date fixed, when plaintiff has notice that performance is refused. The suit is filed well within the prescribed limitation period.

FACTS OF THE CASE

6. On 10.01.2024, the Plaintiff entered into an Agreement to Sell with the Defendant for the purchase of residential Flat No. 302, 3rd Floor, Sunshine Apartments, Koramangala, Bengaluru, bearing Survey Number {{PROPERTY_SURVEY_NUMBER}}, measuring 1200 sq ft, for a total consideration of Rs. 85,00,000/-. The said Agreement to Sell is marked as Annexure A.

7. Pursuant to the said Agreement, the Plaintiff paid an advance amount of Rs. 25,00,000/- to the Defendant on 10.01.2024 via RTGS (UTR: HDFC20240110987654). The receipt of the said payment is marked as Annexure B.

8. The remaining balance consideration of Rs. 60,00,000/- was agreed to be paid by the Plaintiff on or before 10.07.2024 against the execution and registration of the Sale Deed.

9. The Plaintiff has always been ready and willing to perform her part of the contract and had arranged the balance funds to complete the transaction within the stipulated timeline.

10. The Plaintiff resides at Indiranagar, Bengaluru and is by occupation {{PLAINTIFF_OCCUPATION}}, while the Defendant resides at HSR Layout, Bengaluru and is by occupation {{DEFENDANT_OCCUPATION}}.

BREACH OF CONTRACT

11. On 05.06.2024, the Defendant sent a letter refusing to execute the Sale Deed, claiming he had received a higher offer for the property, thereby committing a breach of the Agreement to Sell. The said refusal letter is marked as Annexure D.

12. The Plaintiff issued a legal notice dated 20.06.2024 calling upon the Defendant to execute the Sale Deed as per the Agreement, but the Defendant failed to comply and reiterated his refusal to perform the contract. The legal notice with postal receipt is marked as Annexure C.

DAMAGES

13. Due to the Defendant's wrongful refusal and breach of contract, the Plaintiff is deprived of her rightful title to the suit property and has been compelled to approach this Hon'ble Court, entitling her to specific performance of the contract and recovery of costs incurred.

LEGAL BASIS

14. The Plaintiff is entitled to a decree of specific performance of the agreement under Sections 10 and 14 of the Specific Relief Act, 1963, the subject matter being such that the grant of compensation in money would not afford adequate relief.

15. The Plaintiff has been ready and willing to perform her part of the agreement at all material times within the meaning of Section 16(c) of the Specific Relief Act, 1963.

16. The present suit is maintainable under Section 9 of the Code of Civil Procedure, 1908.

CAUSE OF ACTION

17. The cause of action for the present suit first arose on 05.06.2024 when the Defendant committed the breach / wrongful act giving rise to the Plaintiff's right to institute the present suit.

18. The cause of action further arose when the Defendant, despite receipt of the legal notice dated 20.06.2024, failed and refused to comply with the Plaintiff's demand.

19. The cause of action is not a continuing cause of action. The breach consisted of a single event. However, the Plaintiff's right to damages subsists.

VALUATION AND COURT FEE

20. The suit is valued at Rs. {{TOTAL_SUIT_VALUE}}/- (Rupees {{TOTAL_SUIT_VALUE_WORDS}} Only) for the purposes of jurisdiction and court fee. Appropriate court fee has been paid on this plaint under the provisions of the Karnataka Court Fees and Suits Valuation Act, 1958 as applicable in the State of Karnataka. The plaint is properly stamped.

INTEREST

21. The Plaintiff claims interest on the suit amount as follows:

    (a) Pendente lite interest at such rate as this Hon'ble Court deems fit and just from the date of filing of this suit till the date of judgment / decree, under Section 34 of the Code of Civil Procedure, 1908;
    (b) Future interest at such rate as this Hon'ble Court deems fit and just from the date of judgment / decree till the date of realisation of the entire decretal amount.

PRAYER

In the premises aforesaid, the Plaintiff most respectfully prays that this Hon'ble Court be pleased to:

    (a) Pass a decree of specific performance directing the Defendant to perform and complete the agreement dated 10.01.2024 in favour of the Plaintiff;
    (b) Direct the Defendant to execute and register the necessary conveyance / sale deed in favour of the Plaintiff upon receipt of the balance consideration;
    (c) In the alternative, award compensation / damages in lieu of specific performance;
    (d) Award costs of the suit;
    (e) Grant such other and further relief(s) as this Hon'ble Court may deem fit and proper in the facts and circumstances of the case.

LIST OF DOCUMENTS

Annexure A — Original Agreement to Sell dated 10.01.2024

Annexure B — RTGS receipt (UTR: HDFC20240110987654)

Annexure C — Legal notice dated 20.06.2024 with postal receipt

Annexure D — Defendant's refusal letter dated 05.06.2024

VERIFICATION

Verified at Bengaluru on this the ____ day of __________ 20____.

I, Anita Sharma, the Plaintiff above-named, do hereby verify that the contents of paragraphs 1 to 13 of this plaint are true and correct to my knowledge, and no material facts have been concealed therefrom, and that I have not filed any other suit, petition, or proceeding in any court or before any authority in respect of the same subject matter and cause of action.

DEPONENT

Anita Sharma

PLAINTIFF

                                    THROUGH:
                                    {{ADVOCATE_NAME}}
                                    Advocate for the Plaintiff
                                    Enrollment No. {{ENROLLMENT_NO}}
                                    {{ADVOCATE_ADDRESS}}
                                    Mobile: {{ADVOCATE_PHONE}}
                                    Email: {{ADVOCATE_EMAIL}}

scenarios.name: Breach of Contract — Damages (Business)

checks.detail: Total claim Rs.32L or breakdown stated

scenarios.draft_text:
IN THE COURT OF THE COMMERCIAL COURT (COMMERCIAL DIVISION)
AT BENGALURU

COMMERCIAL SUIT NO. __________ / 20____

TECHSOFT SOLUTIONS PVT LTD,
A company registered under the Companies Act, 2013,
Having its registered office at: MG Road, Bengaluru,
Represented by its Director Priya Menon,
... PLAINTIFF

VERSUS

CLOUDBRIDGE SYSTEMS LLP,
A limited liability partnership registered under the LLP Act, 2008,
Having its office at: Whitefield, Bengaluru,
Represented by its Designated Partner Vikram Singh,
... DEFENDANT

SUIT FOR DAMAGES FOR BREACH OF CONTRACT WITH INTEREST AND COSTS

MOST RESPECTFULLY SHOWETH:

COMMERCIAL COURT MAINTAINABILITY

1. The subject matter of the present suit arises out of a commercial dispute within the meaning of Section 2(1)(c) of the Commercial Courts Act, 2015.

2. The suit is valued above the specified value as prescribed under Section 12 of the Commercial Courts Act, 2015, and is therefore maintainable before this Hon'ble Commercial Court.

JURISDICTION

3. The Plaintiff is a person sui juris and competent to institute the present suit. The Defendant is also a person sui juris and can be sued.

4. This Hon'ble Court has territorial jurisdiction to try and entertain the present suit inasmuch as the cause of action arose within the local limits of the jurisdiction of this Hon'ble Court at Bengaluru, and the Defendant carries on business within such territorial limits.

5. This Hon'ble Court has pecuniary jurisdiction to try the present suit, the suit being valued at Rs. 32,00,000/- (Rupees Thirty-Two Lakhs Only), which is within the pecuniary limits prescribed for this Hon'ble Court. This Hon'ble Court has subject matter jurisdiction to entertain commercial disputes under the Commercial Courts Act, 2015.

LIMITATION

6. The present suit is within the period of limitation prescribed under Article 55 of the Schedule to the Limitation Act, 1963, which provides a period of three years from when the contract is broken. The suit is filed well within the prescribed limitation period.

SECTION 12A COMPLIANCE

7. The Plaintiff confirms compliance with Section 12A of the Commercial Courts Act, 2015. Pre-institution mediation was not conducted as the Plaintiff is seeking urgent interim relief for recovery of funds wrongfully retained by the Defendant. Alternatively, if mediation is required, the Plaintiff undertakes to participate in mediation as directed by this Hon'ble Court.

FACTS OF THE CASE

8. On 01.02.2024, the Plaintiff entered into a Software Development Agreement with the Defendant for the development of an ERP system, with a total contract value of Rs. 45,00,000/-. A copy of the said Agreement is annexed hereto as Annexure A.

9. Pursuant to the terms of the Agreement, the Plaintiff paid the first milestone payment of Rs. 20,00,000/- to the Defendant on 01.02.2024 via NEFT bearing UTR No. ICIC20240201456789. A copy of the NEFT receipt is annexed hereto as Annexure B.

10. As per the agreed timeline under the Agreement, the Defendant was obligated to deliver the first module of the ERP system on or before 01.05.2024.

11. The Defendant failed to deliver the first module or any part of the ERP system by the agreed deadline of 01.05.2024, thereby committing breach of the contractual obligations.

12. On 15.06.2024, the Defendant communicated its decision to abandon the project entirely without completing the deliverables, as evidenced by the email correspondence between the parties. A copy of the said email correspondence is annexed hereto as Annexure C.

13. The Plaintiff issued a legal notice dated 01.07.2024 calling upon the Defendant to refund the advance and compensate for losses, but the Defendant has failed to comply with the said notice. A copy of the legal notice is annexed hereto as Annexure E.

DAMAGES

14. The Plaintiff is entitled to the refund of the advance payment of Rs. 20,00,000/- paid to the Defendant, which remains unadjusted due to the non-performance of the contract.

15. The Plaintiff has suffered consequential loss amounting to Rs. 12,00,000/- due to delay in operations caused by the non-delivery of the ERP system, as assessed in the loss assessment report prepared by the auditor. A copy of the loss assessment report is annexed hereto as Annexure D.

16. The Plaintiff claims interest at the rate of 18% per annum on the total claim amount from the date of breach until realisation, owing to the wrongful retention of funds and delayed compensation.

LEGAL BASIS

17. The Defendant has committed a breach of the agreement by failing to perform the obligations undertaken therein, thereby entitling the Plaintiff to claim damages under Section 73 of the Indian Contract Act, 1872.

18. The Defendant has committed a repudiatory breach by refusing to perform its obligations, entitling the Plaintiff to treat the contract as at an end and seek appropriate relief.

19. The Plaintiff is entitled to compensation under Section 73 of the Indian Contract Act, 1872, which provides that when a contract has been broken, the party who suffers by such breach is entitled to receive compensation for any loss or damage caused thereby, which naturally arose in the usual course of things from such breach, or which the parties knew, when they made the contract, to be likely to result from the breach of it.

20. The present suit is maintainable under Section 9 of the Code of Civil Procedure, 1908.

CAUSE OF ACTION

21. The cause of action for the present suit first arose on 01.05.2024 when the Defendant failed to deliver the first module by the agreed deadline, constituting breach of contract.

22. The cause of action further arose on 15.06.2024 when the Defendant abandoned the project and refused to complete the deliverables.

23. The cause of action is a continuing cause of action as the Defendant continues to wrongfully retain the advance payment without performance.

VALUATION AND COURT FEE

24. The suit is valued at Rs. 32,00,000/- (Rupees Thirty-Two Lakhs Only) for the purposes of jurisdiction and court fee. Court fee of Rs. {{COURT_FEE_AMOUNT}} has been paid on this plaint under the provisions of the Karnataka Court Fees and Suits Valuation Act, 1958, calculated at the applicable rate on the suit value. The plaint is properly stamped.

INTEREST

25. The Plaintiff claims interest on the suit amount as follows:
    (a) Interest at 18% per annum from 01.05.2024 (date of breach) until realisation;
    (b) Pendente lite interest at such rate as this Hon'ble Court deems fit and just from the date of filing of this suit till the date of judgment/decree, under Section 34 of the Code of Civil Procedure, 1908;
    (c) Future interest at such rate as this Hon'ble Court deems fit and just from the date of judgment/decree till the date of realisation of the entire decretal amount.

PRAYER

In the premises aforesaid, the Plaintiff most respectfully prays that this Hon'ble Court be pleased to:

    (a) Pass a decree in favour of the Plaintiff and against the Defendant for a sum of Rs. 32,00,000/- (Rupees Thirty-Two Lakhs Only) towards damages comprising Rs. 20,00,000/- refund of advance and Rs. 12,00,000/- consequential damages;
    (b) Award interest at 18% per annum from 01.05.2024 until realisation;
    (c) Award pendente lite and future interest at such rate as this Hon'ble Court deems just under Section 34 of the Code of Civil Procedure, 1908;
    (d) Award costs of the suit including costs of the legal notice;
    (e) Grant such other and further relief(s) as this Hon'ble Court may deem fit and proper in the facts and circumstances of the case.

SCHEDULE OF DAMAGES/PARTICULARS OF DAMAGES

The Plaintiff claims the following heads of damages with computation methodology:

26. Actual Loss (Refund of Advance)
    Amount claimed: Rs. 20,00,000/-
    Basis/methodology: First milestone payment under Software Development Agreement dated 01.02.2024
    Documentary proof: NEFT receipt (Annexure B), Software Development Agreement (Annexure A)

27. C
